program_outcomes: Demonstrate the ability to collect, analyze, and interpret data from diverse sources to support evidence-based decision-making within organizations.
Apply a systematic approach to the design, development, and implementation of information systems that align with organizational goals and strategies.
Apply problem-solving techniques to business scenarios/cases by developing action plans aligned with organizational goals.
Create effective written communication (communicate effectively in written form).
Deliver/give oral presentations on business topics, effectively conveying key messages.
Generate innovative solutions to business challenges through creative thinking.
Examine ethical dilemmas and social responsibility issues facing businesses, proposing ethically sound, sustainable courses of action.
Apply quantitative methods to analyze business data, deriving actionable insights to address practical challenges.

courses.name: Experiencing Business in Society I
courses.purpose: This course aims to integrate experiential learning practices to issues of business and society with the expectation that such techniques can create a positive experience, enhancing learning potential.
courses.description: Topics covered during the lectures will be drawn from a pool of issues faced by firms and their managers in their daily practices. The course will cover economics, globalization, international trade, environment, culture and values, communication, business skills, innovation and technology, corporate social responsibility and ethics.
learning_outcomes: Develop skills in finding, collecting, sorting, analyzing and interpreting information on business environment.
Explain and critically assess the relationship between social issues (globalization, culture, ethics…) and business practices.
Relate business decisions and strategies with environmental impacts.
Relate basics of economics with business environment.
Understand the role of communication in personal, organizational and cultural levels.
Develop interpersonal communication skills by getting involved in team work.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: History of Business in the World & in Turkey
Hafta 9: Entrepreneurship and Small Business Ownership
Hafta 10: Business Ethics and Corporate Social Responsibility
Hafta 11: Recent Trends in Business Life
resources: Bovee&Thill, Business in Action, 10, Prentice Hall, 2023, 9780138188498
Karen Collins, Exploring Business, Pearson Prentice Hall, 2008. (selected chapters)
Williams, Sawyer & Berston, Business: A Practical Introduction, Prentice Hall, 2013.
Ebert & Griffin, Business Essentials, Prentice Hall, 2017.
prerequisites: BUS 101 / CDM 204 / HM 118 / LBUS 179 / PUB 205 / THM 103 / VOC 101

courses.name: Introduction to Economics I
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to the basic principles and practice of economics, with a focus on how economists analyze consumer and producer behavior.
courses.description: This is an introductory microeconomics course. The way economists explain consumer and producer behavior is the main focus of the course.
learning_outcomes: Describe firm behavior under different market structures
Find optimal price-quantity policies for firms
Explain the effects of a government intervention to markets
Provide a theoretical explanation for the equilibrium market price of a commodity
Explain the effects of a change in an external factor on markets
Explain the relationship between sustainability, scarcity, and choice in economics, and how limited resources influence decision-making processes.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Principles and Practice of Economics
Hafta 2: Principles and Practice of Economics
Hafta 3: Optimization: Doing the Best You Can
Hafta 4: Optimization: Doing the Best You Can
Hafta 12: Perfect Competition and the Invisible Hand
Hafta 13: Perfect Competition and the Invisible Hand
prerequisites: EC 149 / LEC 101 / PUB 252 / PUB 253 / VEC 111 / VOC 205

courses.name: English for Academic Purposes I
courses.purpose: This course is designed to develop the academic language and academic skills needed by students to be successful in their respective departments. It also aims to build students’ confidence in handling academic texts and communicating information in academic settings.
courses.description: Students are guided through the stages of academic writing, including planning, researching, collecting, and organizing information. The course works with semi-authentic to authentic academic texts and emphasizes reading strategies, academic writing, listening, speaking, and reading skills. It also develops test-taking, paraphrasing, summarizing, and note-taking skills.
learning_outcomes: recognize vocabulary and discern meaning from context
apply note-taking strategies for study
write a summary
contextualize an academic text
deliver a presentation to a specific audience
weekly_topics: Hafta 5: Reading Skills: Contextualizing a text
Hafta 6: Reading Skills: Contextualizing a text

courses.name: Algorithms and Programming
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip engineering students who are not specializing in computing with the necessary skills to design and implement simple computer programs of practical use in a popular programming language, Python, to solve mathematical and scientific problems. It also aims to overcome the fear of writing a program from scratch by teaching a design method that breaks program construction into manageable steps.
courses.description: The course covers the fundamentals of Python programming, including variables, expressions, statements, functions, control statements, iteration, strings, sequences, dictionaries, sets, files, exceptions, classes, objects, NumPy-based array-oriented programming, random processes, and basic software engineering best practices.
learning_outcomes: Understand the fundamental programming concepts and methodologies which are essential to building good python programs.
Demonstrate an understanding of primitive data types, values, operators and expressions in Python.
Apply good programming principles: specify contracts, purpose, example sets, implement and test using appropriate documentation and testing tools.
Analyze engineering problems and gain an approach to use skills with support of practical session.
Use open-source libraries to process and visualize data.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: The Way of the Program: Introduction to Python Programming
Hafta 14: Random and Analysis of Algorithms
Hafta 15: Basic software engineering best practices.
resources: Michael Driscoll; Python 101, 1st edition (June 2, 2014)
Learning Python, O'Reilly Media; 5th ed.
Allen Downey, Think Python, O’Reilly. 2016
prerequisites: CMPE 100 / CMPE 102 / CMPE 169 / COMP 111 / COMP 149 / COMP 179 / FM 149 / MIS 102
CMPE 101 / CMPE 170 / COMP 112 / COMP 150 / COMP 180 / FM 150

courses.name: Calculus I
courses.description: Functions and their properties: inverse functions; exponential and logarithmic functions and their properties; limits and continuity; derivatives and their applications; curve sketching; optimization problems; financial applications; matrices and determinants; and solving linear systems using the Gaussian elimination method.
learning_outcomes: Students learn about functions and their properties, including exponential and logarithmic functions.
Calculations of compound and continuously compounded interest, future and present values of annuities, and amortization.
Students will learn the definition and properties of limits, as well as methods for calculating limits.
Students will learn about continuity, derivatives, and their applications.
Students learn Mean Value Theorem, Intermediate Value Theorem and Rolles Theorem and their applications.
Matrices, determinants, and systems of linear equations. Solving systems of linear equations using the Gaussian elimination method.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Inverse functions, exponential and logarithmic functions and their properties.
Hafta 3: Compound and continuously compounded interest calculations.
Hafta 4: Future and present values of annuities and amortization.
Hafta 12: Continuing applications of derivatives, optimization problems, and curve sketching.
Hafta 13: Matrices, determinants, and systems of linear equations.
Hafta 14: Solving systems of linear equations using the Gaussian elimination method.
resources: Barnett, Raymond A., Ziegler, Michael R. etc. College Mathematics for Business, Economics, Life Sciences, and Social Sciences, Global Edition

courses.name: Turkish Language I
courses.purpose: This lecture aims to help students who have completed their undergraduate studies understand the structure and functionality of the Turkish language from the perspective of the relationship between language and thought, and to develop the ability to use Turkish correctly and effectively as a written and spoken expression tool.
courses.description: The course covers the historical process of the Turkish language, its place in universal language families, important shifts and transformations in Turkish, and dictation rules. It also introduces basic knowledge and practical skills related to the structure and use of the language.
learning_outcomes: Understanding the fundamental concepts of the Turkish language, its historical process, and reflecting this knowledge in daily life.
Comparing Turkish with other languages, identifying problems in this comparison, and responding rationally to such issues.
Learning the structural and conceptual aspects of the Turkish language and improving the ability to evaluate, analyze, and conduct accurate research in this field.
Understanding the relationship between language and other disciplines and using the language with awareness and originality.
Caring about the oral and written rules of the Turkish language and raising awareness.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course description. E-mail writing ethics.
Hafta 4: The emergence of writing alphabets
Hafta 6: Language Classifications and Properties of Turkish
Hafta 13: Spelling rules III Official correspondence rules
Hafta 14: Oral expression types and communication skills.
resources: http://www.sanatblog.com/babil-kuleleri/
Aksan, D. (2017). Dil, Şu Büyülü Düzen…. Ankara: Bilgi.
Korkmaz, Z., Ercilasun, A., Gülensoy, T., Parlatır, İ., Zülfikar, H. & Birinci, N. (2007). Türk dili ve kompozisyon. (2. Baskı). Ankara: Ekin Yayıncılık.
Gülensoy, T. (2001). Türkçe El Kitabı. Ankara: Akçağ.
Aksan, D. (1990). Türkçenin gücü. Ankara: Bilgi Yayınevi.
Aksoy, Ö. A. (1978). Atasözleri ve deyimler sözlüğü I-II. Ankara: TDK Yayınları.
Bilgin, M. (2006). Anlamdan anlatıma Türkçemiz. Ankara: Anı Yayınları.
Muallimoğlu, N. (2005). Bütün Yönleriyle Hitabet Konuşma Sanatı. İstanbul: Avcıol Yayınları.
Dijital kaynak : www.tdk.gov.tr
Korkmaz, Z. (2008).Gramer Terimleri Sözlüğü. Ankara: TDK.

courses.name: Intermediate Programming
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students who are not specializing in computing with the necessary skills to design and write simple computer programs of practical use in a commercially popular programming language. It also aims to help students overcome the fear of writing a program from scratch by introducing a step-by-step design method and to develop good programming habits through specifications, documentation, and testing.
courses.description: The course covers the fundamentals of programming using Python, including program specification and design, identifiers and types, expressions, selection and iteration, methods, program decomposition, classes, constructors and objects, string processing, arrays, map/filter/fold patterns, sorting, searching, recursive algorithms, and basic software engineering best practices.
learning_outcomes: Take an informal problem statement and clarify it, produce a formal specification, define data. Explain the process including specification, editing, compilation, testing, documentation, delivery.
Recognize, write and, when incorrect, correct the syntax of method/procedure, expression and identifier declarations.
Recognize, construct and correct, when incorrect, expressions involving the data types for Boolean values, exact and inexact numbers, arrays and text strings.
Evaluate the results of the application of the scope rules of the language as they affect method/procedure parameters/arguments and method/procedure local variables.
Decompose the solution for a formally specified problem into methods. Specify contracts, purpose, example sets and test and templates for methods using appropriate documentation and testing tools.
Recognize which method or procedure templates, including selection, definite and indefinite iterative and recursive patterns are appropriate to particular tasks and use them.
Gain an approach to engineering problems and to use skills with support of practical session.
Analyze engineering problems and be able to identify and correct information and technical shortcomings.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: History of computers and programming. The programming process: specification, design, compilation, testing, deployment.
Hafta 2: Identifiers and types. The process of specifying a program from an informal description and data definitions.
Hafta 3: Expressions: arithmetic, arithmetic/logical, logical. Creating examples for a program specification and planning tests.

Hafta 4: Designing programs that choose: an if ...else if ... else pattern. Creating and refining templates using logical elimination. Designing programs that repeat definitely: a for pattern. Designing programs that repeat definitely: a while pattern.
Hafta 6: Methods with conditional and iterative statements. Suitable test cases for definite conditional and iteration methods.
Hafta 8: Program decomposition and helper methods. The “one control construct per method” heuristic. Specifications and contracts for helper methods.
Hafta 10: Strings: manipulating text. Patterns for String processing. Splitting text. Using regular expressions.
Hafta 11: Arrays and the mutative nature of arrays.
Hafta 12: The extent of array variables.
Hafta 13: Map, filter and fold patterns for arrays.
Hafta 14: Efficient sorting, searching and introducing recursive algorithms.
Hafta 15: Basic software engineering best practices.
resources: Bader, D. (2017). Python Tricks: The Book - A Buffet of Awesome Python Features. Dan Bader.
Bhargava, A. Y. (2016). Grokking Algorithms: An Illustrated Guide for Programmers and Other Curious People. Manning Publications.
Ramalho, L. (2022). Fluent Python: Clear, Concise, and Effective Programming (2nd ed.). O'Reilly Media.
Gorelick, M., & Ozsvald, I. (2020). High Performance Python: Practical Performant Programming for Humans (2nd ed.). O'Reilly Media.

courses.name: Office Applications for Business and Economics
courses.purpose: This course is designed to help students succeed in their studies and in their jobs after university by introducing them to essential office applications.
courses.description: This is an introductory course in the use of MS Word, MS PowerPoint, and MS Excel.
learning_outcomes: Create MS PowerPoint presentations and MS Word documents.
Format and manage worksheets in MS Excel.
Learn fundamental formulas and functions in MS Excel to organize data for your needs.
Create charts and draw graphs in MS Excel.
Learn how to make inferences using data analysis tools in MS Excel.
Learn fundamental concepts about MS PowerPoint and MS Excel.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Creating and Editing a Presentation in MS PowerPoint
Hafta 3: Introduction to MS Excel Worksheets
Hafta 4: Formulas, Functions, and Formatting in MS Excel
Hafta 5: Working with Large Worksheets in MS Excel
Hafta 6: Creating, Sorting, and Querying a Table in MS Excel
Hafta 7: No Classes Due to the Midterm Week
Hafta 8: Working with PivotTables in MS Excel
Hafta 10: Advanced Concepts in MS Excel
Hafta 11: Introduction to APA Style Referencing
Hafta 12: Creating a Research Paper with References and Sources using MS Word
Hafta 13: Creating a Document with a Table of Contents, Tables, and Figures using MS Word
resources: Vermaat, M. E., Freund, S. M., Hoisington, C., Schmieder, E., & Last, M. Z. (2016). Shelly Cashman Series Microsoft Office 365 & Office 2016: Introductory. Nelson Education.
Walkenbach, J. (2015). Microsoft Excel 2016 Bible: The comprehensive tutorial resource. Wiley Publishing.

courses.name: Experiencing Business in Society II
courses.purpose: This course aims to study the issues of business and society with an expectation to create a positive, eager classroom climate that links individual and group insights to the theoretical issues studied. It also helps students connect business and management concepts with managerial decision-making in finance, marketing, and strategy.
courses.description: This course focuses on an in-depth analysis of business and management issues that incorporate managerial decision-making in finance, marketing, and strategy. Students work in teams and practice the world of business and management while thinking and acting as managers at various levels of the management hierarchy.
learning_outcomes: Explain management functions (planning, organizing, directing and controlling) and their relations with each other.
Understand the production process and operations functions of a business.
Identify the key concepts of marketing.
Identify the basic concepts and relationships in financial markets.
Discuss the factors that influence buyer behavior and describe the external marketing environment in which businesses operate.
Understand the internet, electronic commerce, the nature of internet marketing and strategies, structures and processes and evaluate their contribution to new business environment.
Develop interpersonal communication skills by getting involved in team work.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Functions of Management: The aim of this module is to identify the functions of management, understand the process by which a company develops and implements a strategic plan and discuss forms of business.
Hafta 2: Strategy Development: The aim of this module is to discuss the key issues related to strategy and strategy development.
Hafta 3: Entrepreneurship: The aim of this module is to provide students with an understanding of the stages and challenges of starting a business.
Hafta 4: Production: The aim of this module is to define the "product" and explain where the product ideas come from and describe the process of developing a product that meets customer needs.

Hafta 5: Marketing: The aim of this module is to define the terms "marketing" and "marketing strategy", and outline the tasks involved in selecting a target market and researching a marketing concept.

Hafta 6: Marketing Research: This module examines how marketing research is used to support marketing decisions. It discusses ways in which the information used to make marketing decisions is gathered and analysed.
Hafta 7: Integrated Marketing Communication: The aim of this module is to provide an understanding of marketing communications as not a single tool but, rather, a mix of several tools.
Hafta 9: E-business: In this module, the effects of internet and e-commerce on business practices are discussed.
Hafta 10: Teamwork and Leadership: In this module, the importance of teamwork and leadership in business life is discussed.
Hafta 11: Human Resources Management: The aim of the module is to define human resource management and explain how managers develop and implement a human resource plan.
Hafta 12: Accounting: The aim of this module is to define accounting and learn how to use basic financial statements to understand about the financial performance of the firm.

Hafta 13: Finance: This module aims to identify the functions of money, describe the Central Bank policies to control money supply, define financial institutions and instruments, and explain the role of a financial manager in a firm.
resources: Courtland L. Bovee / John V. Thill, Business in Action, 10th edition, Prentice Hall, 2023, ISBN: 9780138188498
prerequisites: BUS 101 / CDM 204 / HM 118 / LBUS 180 / PUB 205 / THM 103 / VOC 101

courses.name: Introduction to Economics II
courses.purpose: This is an introductory macroeconomics course. The aim of the course is to teach elementary macroeconomic analysis and its applications.
courses.description: Among the main topics discussed are national income accounting, aggregate demand and supply, unemployment, money and inflation.
learning_outcomes: Calculate important macroeconomic concepts such as the CPI or GDP
Explain economic cycles by the theories proposed by different schools of thought in economics
Explain unemployment through the labor market
Explain the effects of fiscal and monetary policies
Explain the relation between important economic concepts such as inflation rate, interest rate, unemployment rate etc.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: The Wealth of Nations: Defining and Measuring Macroeconomic Aggregates
Hafta 2: The Wealth of Nations: Defining and Measuring Macroeconomic Aggregates
prerequisites: EC 150 / LEC 102 / PUB 252 / PUB 254 / VEC 112 / VOC 206

courses.name: English for Academic Purposes II
courses.purpose: This course is designed to further develop and expand students’ academic language skills so they can succeed in their academic departments. It also aims to increase students’ confidence and comprehensibility when delivering information in an academic setting.
courses.description: Students are guided through the stages of academic writing, including planning, researching, collecting, and organizing information. The course works with semi-authentic to authentic academic texts and emphasizes efficient reading strategies, academic writing, listening, speaking, and reading skills. Paraphrasing, summarizing, and note-taking are also further developed.
learning_outcomes: recognize vocabulary and discern meaning from context
design data collection strategies
implement data collection strategies
write a report
write a reflection

courses.name: Calculus II
courses.description: Riemann integration. The substitution rule and integration by parts. The area between curves. Additional integration techniques. Improper integrals. Ordinary differential equations. Multivariable functions. The method of Lagrange multipliers. Applications in business and economics. Sequences, series, and their applications in business and economics.
learning_outcomes: Upon satisfactory completion of this course, students should: have a theoretical background such as partial derivatives, integration, series, sequences and differential equations.
be able to apply basic knowledge of these concepts to related fields
have a basic knowledge of methods of integration
be able to apply knowledge of basic rules in order to solve differential equations
be able to learn the application of Differential Equations.
be able to learn multivariable functions and differentiation, the method of Lagrange multipliers, and the least squares method
have the ability to analyze series and sequences and how they relate to business and economic issues.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Antiderivatives, Indefinite Integral, Integration by Substitution.
Hafta 3: The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus: Areas Between Curves.
Hafta 4: Integration by parts, other integration methods.
Hafta 5: More on other integration methods.
Hafta 6: Applications in Business and Economics.
Hafta 9: Functions of Several Variables, Partial Derivatives.
Hafta 11: Maxima and Minima using Lagrange Multipliers (with constraints).
Hafta 12: Method of Least Squares, Double Integrals over Rectangular Regions
Hafta 13: Double Integrals over More General Regions, Sequences
Hafta 14: Series, Summation, and Arithmetic and Geometric Sequences and Their Application.

courses.name: Turkish Language II
courses.purpose: This lecture aims to teach students who have completed their undergraduate studies the academic and literary verbal features of the Turkish language, help them reach a certain intellectual level that enables them to evaluate and discuss these features, and learn the historical development of literature in this field.
courses.description: The course covers written forms of expression, types of written expression, travel writings, memoirs, fictional narrative, literary terms, the story as a literary genre, the novel genre and Turkish novel, theater, and poetry.
learning_outcomes: Improving writing skills in Turkish language by learning the fundamental concepts and spelling rules.
Conceptualizing written expressions in the Turkish language and acquiring the ability to understand the differences between types of expression.
Learning the basics of written expressions, comparing expression types, and reflecting this knowledge into personal work in the future.
Carrying the knowledge gained in this class into professional and academic life, improving expression, critical thinking, and written command.
Acquiring originality in thinking and demonstrating and analyzing literary pieces with critical thinking abilities.
weekly_topics: Hafta 9: Story as a literary genre.
Hafta 10: Novel genre and Turkish novel.
resources: Bilgin, M. (2006). Anlamdan Anlatıma Türkçemiz. Ankara: Anı Yayıncılık.
Özdemir, E. (2005). Sözlü-Yazılı Anlatım Sanatı Kompozisyon. İstanbul: Remzi Kitabevi.
TDK. Türk Dil Kurumu Güncel Türkçe Sözlük. Erişim Tarihi: 07.01.2020. https://sozluk.gov.tr/

Birinci, N. (2015). Yazılı Kompozisyon Türleri. Yüksek Öğretim Türk Dili Dersleri “Çerçeve Programı”na Uygun ve TDK “İmlâ Kılavuzu”na Uygun Türk Dili ve Kompozisyon içinde. Bursa: Ekin Basım Yayım Dağıtım.
Akyol, H. (2006). Yeni Programa Uygun Türkçe Öğretim Yöntemleri. Ankara: Kök Yayıncılık.
Özkan Mustafa, Tören Hatice, Esin Osman. Yüksek Öğretimde Türk Dili Yazılı ve Sözlü Anlatım, Filiz Kitabevi, İstanbul (2.Baskı) 2006.
Aksan, D. (1993), Şiir Dili ve Türk Şiir Dili, İstanbul: Şafak Matbaacılık.
And, M. (2014). Başlangıcından 1983’e Türk Tiyatro Tarihi. İstanbul: İletişim Yayınları.
Bates, H. E. (2013). Kısa Öykü: Yazınsal Bir Tür Olarak. (Çev. Gökçen Ezber) İstanbul: Bilge Kültür Sanat Yayınları.
Özdemir, E. (1983), Yazı ve Yazınsal Türler, İstanbul: Varlık Yayınları.
Karataş, T. (2004), Ansiklopedik Edebiyat Terimleri Sözlüğü, Ankara: Akçağ Yay.
YÖK Yayın Etiği (2019). https://www.yok.gov.tr/Sayfalar/Kurumsal/mevzuat/bilimselarastirma-ve-etik-yonetmeligi.aspx

courses.name: Business Information Systems
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with knowledge of the structure of business information systems and how they are designed to support management decision-making.
courses.description: The course covers methods of processing data and their business applications, database construction and basic querying, approaches to the design and development of business information systems, and legal, ethical, and organizational issues related to the security and control of business data. It also addresses selecting an appropriate system for a given business need.
learning_outcomes: Use the systems approach to identify and define business problems.
Develop basic skills in system analysis and in solving business problems.
Identify the information needs of individuals, processes, and organizations.
Create simple models of organizational data and processes.
Use the elements of the information technology infrastructure to fulfill the information needs.
Select the appropriate systems development methodology to satisfy a particular set of information needs.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Business Information Systems in Global Business Today
Hafta 3: Information Sytems, Organizations and Strategy
Hafta 4: Ethical and Social Issues in Information Systems
Hafta 5: IT Infrastructure and Emerging Technologies
Hafta 6: Foundations of BI: Databases and Information Management
Hafta 8: Telecommunications, the Internet, and Wireless Technology Securing Information Systems
Hafta 10: E-Commerce: Digital Markets, Digital Goods
resources: Laudon, K. C., & Laudon, J. P. (2022). Management Information Systems: Managing the Digital Firm (17th ed.). Pearson.
O'Brien, J. A., & Marakas, G. M. (2019). Management Information Systems (11th ed.). McGraw-Hill Education.
Stair, R. M., & Reynolds, G. W. (2020). Principles of Information Systems (14th ed.). Cengage Learning.

courses.name: Introduction to Accounting
courses.purpose: The aim of the course is to enable students to appreciate the role of accounting in the business environment, to develop the skills required to record business transactions and to apply accounting concepts in the preparation of financial statements.
courses.description: Upon successful completion of this course, students should be able to demonstrate an understanding of the purpose of accounting, the application of concepts and conventions, mastery of the procedures for recording business events and compiling a financial database and preparation of financial statements for managerial decisions.
learning_outcomes: Define function and role of accounting in business
Identify business transactions which affect the financial position of the company
Calculate the effects of transactions on the financial position of the company
Prepare the basic financial statements of a company
Demonstrate an understanding of basic accounting principles
Apply basic accounting principles in accounting for transactions
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Definition and Purpose of Accounting; Accounting Information and User Groups
Hafta 2: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles; The Accounting Equation
Hafta 3: Measurement Issues in Accounting; Accounts and Chart of Accounts; Rules of Debit & Credit
Hafta 4: General Journal and Journal Entries; Preparing the Trial Balance (I)
Hafta 5: Accrual Accounting and the Adjusting Entries (I)
Hafta 6: Accrual Accounting and the Adjusting Entries (II)
Hafta 12: Preparing the financial statements of Merchandising Companies
Hafta 13: Short term liquid assets; Cash and marketable Securities
Hafta 14: Accounting for Receivables; Valuation and allowance for doubtful accounts
resources: M. Powers, B. Needles, S. Crosson, PRINCIPLES OF ACCOUNTING, 12, South-Western Cengage Learning
prerequisites: BKS 101 / BUS 105 / CUL 310 / HEC 241 / HM 111 / MUG 101 / SHU 102 / THM 207 / VOC 221

courses.name: Statistical Analysis for Business
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to provide an understanding of statistical tools that can be useful in a business environment. The main focus of the course is to provide an understanding of the statistical concepts that provide the basis of further statistical analysis.
courses.description: The course covers descriptive statistics, data analysis (graphical descriptions of data, numerical descriptive measures), probability, random variables (discrete random variables and continuous random variables), probability distribution, sampling distribution, point estimation and interval estimation. Students will also learn to prepare and analyze data using appropriate software at computer labs during class hours.
learning_outcomes: Collect data for specific problems and avoid problems leading to biased sample data.
Explain the purpose of descriptive and inductive methods in applications.
Summarize the characteristics (location, variation, shape) of a distribution.
Identify basic stochastic models behind observations.
Calculate probabilities related to daily business problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Location, variation and shape of a distribution, part 1
Hafta 5: Location, variation and shape of a distribution, part 2
Hafta 6: Stochastic models behind the observations
Hafta 12: More about random variables and their distributions, part 1
Hafta 13: More about random variables and their distributions, part 2
Hafta 14: Estimation: Point estimation; confidence intervals, part 1
Hafta 15: Estimation: Point estimation; confidence intervals, part 2
resources: Devlet Istatistik Enstitusu (editor): Istatistik Terimleri Sözlüğü, Ankara, 1998.
Newbold, Paul: Statistics for Business and Economics, fourth edition. Prentice Hall, 1995.
Ozdemir, Durmuş: Applied Statistics for Economics and Business. Istanbul Bilgi University Press, 2001.
prerequisites: CMN 168 / EC 271 / ENGR 240 / HEC 213 / IE 231 / IR 213 / LOT 211 / MATH 240 / PSY 213 / SOC 213
EC 176 / MATH 106 / MATH 108 / MATH 110 / MATH 150 / MATH 156 / MATH 162 / MATH 169 / MATH 176

courses.name: History of Turkish Revolution I
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce key concepts such as social science, history, revolution, counter-revolution, coups d’etat, state, political power, authority, law, and rights. It also aims to help students better understand the political, social, and economic structure of Ottoman society as a basis for understanding the conditions of the Turkish Revolution, while encouraging a critical perspective on historical events and multiple viewpoints.
courses.description: The course covers basic concepts related to social science and history, social change, revolution, counter-revolution, coups d’etat, state, law, political power, and authority. It also examines the political, social, and economic structure of the Ottoman Empire, including Ottoman modernization, and discusses the differences between Ottoman land formation and feudalism.
learning_outcomes: To discuss concepts such as social science, history, revolution, coups d'etat, state, political power, authority, law, and rights.
To understand the political, social, and economic structure of the Ottoman Empire.
To understand the differences between Ottoman land formation and feudalism.
To understand what Ottoman modernization was.
To discuss what Ottoman modernization was.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: to define the term of "science", "social science" and "history". to discuss the position of "history" in social sciences.
Hafta 3: to define the term of "social change", "social progress" and "social recession". to define and to discuss the term of “participation” and “income distribution”.
Hafta 4: to study on Marxist and Weberian perspectives which are used to clarify social changes (I).
Hafta 5: to study on Marxist and Weberian perspectives which are used to clarify social changes (II).
Hafta 6: to define the term of “revolution”, "counter revolution" and "coups d’etat".
Hafta 8: to define state and law.
Hafta 9: to define the term of political power and authority.
Hafta 10: to define the political structure of Ottoman Empire (I).
Hafta 11: to define the political structure of Ottoman Empire (II).
Hafta 12: to define the social structure of Ottoman Empire (I).
Hafta 13: to define the social structure of Ottoman Empire (II).
Hafta 14: to define the economic structure of Ottoman Empire (I).
Hafta 15: to define the economic structure of Ottoman Empire (II).
resources: Gülten KAZGAN, Tanzimat’tan 21. Yüzyıla Türkiye Ekonomisi, İstanbul, Bilgi Üniversitesi Yayınları, 2009.
İlhan TEKELİ ve Selim İLKİN, Cumhuriyet’in Harcı I, İstanbul, İstanbul Bilgi Üniversitesi Yayınları, 2003.
İlhan TEKELİ ve Selim İLKİN, Cumhuriyet’in Harcı II, İstanbul, İstanbul Bilgi Üniversitesi Yayınları, 2003.

courses.code: GE Quantitative Reasoning & Critical Thinking List I
categories: GE Quantitative Reasoning & Critical Thinking List I

courses.name: GE Social Science List I
courses.purpose: This elective list offers general education social science courses that help students explore key political, historical, and social issues from different perspectives.
courses.description: This page presents the GE Social Science List I elective pool for 2025-2026, including courses such as state and institutions, EU studies, science-economy-society, world civilizations, political geography, democracy, social movements, sport politics, Olympic Games, governance, French politics and institutions, power-liberty-justice, and global animal history.

courses.name: Further Statistics for Business
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with statistical tools that can be utilized for handling business problems. It builds on the statistical concepts and methods studied in BUS 273.
courses.description: The course focuses on hypothesis testing in general, testing problems for one and two populations, contingency tables and testing for independence, correlation, simple and multiple regression, and analysis of variance. Students will also learn to prepare and analyze data using appropriate software during class hours at computer labs.
learning_outcomes: Estimate a distribution and explain the outcome of the estimation.
Test a statistical hypothesis, using a significance test based on the normal distribution.
Measure the degree of dependence between variables, using chi-square, the odds ratio, and correlation.
Regress one variable on others.
Test for equality of several population means.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Estimation: Point estimation; confidence intervals - single population
Hafta 4: Estimation: Point estimation; confidence intervals - single population
Hafta 5: Estimation: Point estimation; confidence intervals - two population
Hafta 6: Estimation: Point estimation; confidence intervals - two population
Hafta 8: Estimation: Point estimation; confidence intervals - two population
resources: Bowerman, Bruce L., O’Connell, Richard T., & Hand, Michael L.: Business Statistics in Practice, second edition. McGraw Hill, 2001.
Newbold, Paul, Carlson, William L., & Thorne, Betty: Statistics for Business and Economics, fifth edition. Prentice Hall, 2003.
Özdemir, Durmus: Applied Statistics for Economics and Business. Istanbul Bilgi University Press, 2001.
prerequisites: BUS 273 / EC 271 / HEC 213 / IE 231 / IR 213 / LEC 213 / MATH 303 / PSY 213 / SOC 213 / VOC 188

courses.name: Calculus for Economists and Administrative Sciences
courses.description: Systems of Linear Equations, Gauss-Jordan Method. Matrices: Matrix Language, Algebra of matrices, Matrix equations, Inverse Matrix Method for solving systems of linear equations. Linear programming: Geometry of linear inequalities, Graphical method Linear Programming: The Simplex Method. More Matrices: Positive definite matrices, determinants. Lagrange Multipliers and Applications to Optimization Problems.
learning_outcomes: Upon completion of this course, the student will learn matrix, determinant and their properties.
learn the language of Linear programming.
understand langrange multipliers and applicatons to optimization problems.
understand Geometry of linear inequalities, Graphical method and Simplex Method.
understand Dual Simplex Method.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Methods of solving of the linear differential equations.
Hafta 4: Linear Programing: Geometri of lineer inequalities.
Hafta 8: Review of the language of Lineer programing.
Hafta 13: Application Lagrange multipliers to the optimization problems.
Hafta 14: Review of ordinary differential equations.
resources: Calculus / Ross L. Finney, George B. Thomas, Jr. : with the collaboration of Maurice D. Weir.
prerequisites: EC 176 / LMATH 170 / MATH 110 / MATH 156 / MATH 162 / MATH 170 / MATH 176

courses.name: History of Turkish Revolution II
courses.purpose: This course aims to deepen students’ knowledge of the conditions surrounding the Turkish National War, the Turkish Revolution, and the foundation of the Modern Turkish Republic. It also aims to improve students’ critical perspective on history and their understanding of different viewpoints on historical events.
courses.description: The course covers the Turkish National War, the foundation of the Modern Turkish Republic, and the political and social changes that followed its establishment. It also includes key developments related to the end of the Ottoman Empire, the Armistice of Mudros, the Sevres and Lausanne Treaties, the opening of the Turkish Grand National Assembly, and the main stages of the Turkish National War.
learning_outcomes: To understand the reasons for the Turkish National War and to define the Turkish National War.
To compare the Sevres Treaty and the Lausanne Treaty.
To understand and define the foundation of the Modern Turkish Republic.
To understand political and social change after the foundation of the Modern Turkish Republic.
To discuss political and social change after the foundation of the Modern Turkish Republic.
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: 1st World War and Ottoman Empire.
Hafta 5: The Armistice of Moudros and its basic points. Sevres Treaty and its basic points.
Hafta 6: Amasya Notice, Erzurum and Sivas congresses and their basic points. the relationship between İstanbul and Erzurum and Sivas congresses.
Hafta 8: the opening and closing of last Ottoman Parliament. opening of Turkish Grand National Assembly and its main characteristics.
Hafta 11: Turkish foreign policy in Turkish National War.
Hafta 12: Lozan Treaty and its basic points. Lozan Treaty vs Sevres Treaty.
Hafta 13: foundation of modern Turkish Republic (I)
Hafta 14: foundation of modern Turkish Republic (II)
Hafta 15: foundation of modern Turkish Republic (III)
resources: Gülten KAZGAN, Tanzimat’tan 21. Yüzyıla Türkiye Ekonomisi, İstanbul, Bilgi Üniversitesi Yayınları, 2009.
İlhan TEKELİ ve Selim İLKİN, Cumhuriyet’in Harcı I, İstanbul, İstanbul Bilgi Üniversitesi Yayınları, 2003.
İlhan TEKELİ ve Selim İLKİN, Cumhuriyet’in Harcı II, İstanbul, İstanbul Bilgi Üniversitesi Yayınları, 2003.

courses.code: GE Awareness and Social Responsibility List I
courses.name: GE Awareness and Social Responsibility List I
categories: GE Awareness and Social Responsibility List I

courses.name: Year II Summer Practicum
courses.purpose: This practicum is designed to provide students with practical experience in the field of Management Information Systems by placing them in an organization in the ICT sector or in an IT department. It also requires students to complete and report on their summer practice experience.
courses.description: This is the first of the two summer practicums of the Management Information Systems Program. Students are expected to spend a minimum of three weeks at an organization in the Information and Communication Technologies (ITC) sector or at the Information Technology (IT) department of an organization and gain practical experience in the field of Management Information Systems. At the end of the summer practice, students should prepare and submit a report about their experiences to the Program Coordinator.
learning_outcomes: Describe an organization by the sector, industry, legal status, ownership structure and size.
Define the mission of the firm.
Analyze the task environment and primary stakeholders of the firm.
Analyze the organizational structure of the firm.
Analyze the information technology (IT) organization in terms of its structure and processes.
Analyze the IT infrastructure of the firm.
Gain practical experience by participating in system development; installing, upgrading, or using software; installing, upgrading, or using hardware; or training the personnel in the use of IT.
resources: Haag, S., and M. Cummings. (2013). Management Information Systems for the Information Age. 9th Edition. McGraw-Hill.
Laudon, K., and J. Laudon. (2013). Essentials of Management information systems. 10th Ed. Prentice Hall.
Stair, R. M., and G. Reynolds. (2014). Principles of Information Systems. 11th Edition. Course Technology.
Turban E., and L. Volonino. (2011). Information Technology Management. 8th Edition. Wiley.

courses.name: Database Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with up-to-date knowledge and practical skills in database analysis, design, and implementation. It prepares students to understand database concepts, technologies, and systems used in organizational information management.
courses.description: The course covers data modeling and logical database design; data definition, manipulation and control languages; physical database design; SQL programming; standards for database access; transaction management; and client/server systems. It also introduces advanced topics such as object-oriented databases, distributed databases, and data warehousing.
learning_outcomes: Design a relational database.
Implement a relational database.
Query a relational database with the SQL language.
Design multidimensional databases and data warehouses.
Design object-relational and object-oriented databases.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Database Environment, Database Architectures and the Web
Hafta 5: Database System Development Lifecycle, Database Analysis and the DreamHome Case Study
Hafta 9: Methodology–Conceptual Database Design, Methodology–Logical Database Design for the Relational Model
Hafta 10: Methodology–Physical Database Design for Relational Databases, Methodology–Monitoring and Tuning the Operational System
Hafta 14: Web Technology and DBMSs, Semistructured Data and XML
resources: Thomas Connolly, Database Systems: A Practical Approach to Design, Implementation, and Management, 6th ed., Pearson, ISBN 1292061189
Elmasri, R. and S. B. Navathe. Fundamentals of database systems (6th ed.). Addison-Wesley.
Garcia-Molina, H., J. D. Ullman, and J. Widom. Database systems: The complete book (2nd ed.). Prentice Hall.
Gillenson, M. L. Fundamentals of database management systems (2nd ed.). Wiley.
Hoffer, J. A., V. Ramesh, and H. Topi. Modern database management (10th ed.). Prentice Hall.
Johnson, J. L. Database: Models, languages, design. Oxford.
Oppel, A. J. Databases: A beginner’s guide. McGraw-Hill.
Silberschatz, A., H. F. Korth, and H. Sudarshan. Database system concepts (6th ed.). McGraw-Hill.
Date, C. J. Database design and relational theory: Normal forms and all that jazz... O’Reilly Media.
Date, C. J. SQL and relational theory: How to write accurate SQL code (2nd ed.). O’Reilly Media.
Lightstone, S., T. Teorey, and T. Nadeau. Physical database design: The database professional’s guide to exploiting indexes, views, storage, and more. Morgan Kaufmann.
Teorey, T., S. Lightstone, T. Nadeau, and H. V. Jagadish. Database modeling and design: logical design (5th ed.). Morgan Kaufmann.
Harrington, J. L. SQL clearly explained (3rd ed.). Morgan Kaufmann.
Oppel, A., and R. Sheldon. SQL: A beginners guide (3rd ed.). McGraw-Hill.
Rockoff, L. The language of SQL. Course Technology.
prerequisites: CMPE 100 / CMPE 101 / CMPE 102 / CMPE 130 / COMP 112 / COMP 150 / COMP 152 / COMP 180

courses.name: Data Analytics for Business
courses.purpose: This course aims to explain how data contributes to making sound business decisions through the data analytics process. It also aims to build students’ ability to connect business problems with appropriate analytics methods and implement them using a programming language.
courses.description: The course covers the data analytics process from identifying a business problem to disseminating actionable insights throughout the organization. Students will study data preprocessing, analysis with machine learning methods, performance improvement, and the practical use of these concepts through business cases and programming exercises.
learning_outcomes: Define the concepts of data and data analytics.
Identify the company problems that can be solved by data analytics.
Define data preprocessing methods.
Choose appropriate data analytics methods that can solve identified company problems.
Implement methods that will transform data into information with a programming language.
Improve models according to objective metrics through appropriate methods during the data analysis phase.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: The concepts of data and data analytics
Hafta 11: Data analysis - Association & sequential rule mining
Hafta 13: The role of knowledge in decision-making
Hafta 14: Contemporary issues in data analytics
resources: Albright, S. C., & Winston, W. L. (2020). Business Analytics: Data Analysis and Decision Making (7th ed.). Cengage Learning.

Anderson, D. R., Sweeney, D. J., Williams, T. A., Camm, J. D., Cochran, J. J., Fry, M. J., & Ohlmann, J. W. (2019). An Introduction to Management Science: Quantitative Approaches to Decision Making (15th ed.). Cengage Learning.
Camm, J. D., Cochran, J. J., Fry, M. J., Ohlmann, J. W., Anderson, D. R., Sweeney, D. J., & Williams, T. A. (2020). Business Analytics (4th ed.). Cengage Learning.
Evans, J. R. (2019). Business Analytics: Methods, Models, and Decisions (3rd ed.). Pearson.
Shmueli, G., Bruce, P. C., Yahav, I., Patel, N. R., & Lichtendahl Jr., K. C. (2018). Data Mining for Business Analytics: Concepts, Techniques, and Applications with XLMiner (3rd ed.). Wiley.
Han, J., Kamber, M., & Pei, J. (2022). Data Mining: Concepts and Techniques (4th ed.). Morgan Kaufmann.

courses.name: System Analysis and Design I
courses.description: This is the first of the two system analysis and design courses offered by Bilgi's Business Informatics Program. System analysis and design covers the concepts, skills, methodologies, techniques, tools and perspectives essential for systems analysts to successfully develop business information systems. The course approaches system development from a business perspective with a strong emphasis on the role, responsibilities and mindset of the system analyst. It assists in developing the analytical, technical, managerial and social skills required to establish the organizational link between users of the information systems and information technology specialists. The course links all dimensions of system development skills, principles, methodologies, tools and decisions to organizational factors and management of change. After an overview of system development methodologies, Structured System Analysis and Design (SSAD) approach is covered in the course. Students gain first-hand experience on various computer-aided software engineering (CASE) tools and associated methodologies by using them for running cases.
learning_outcomes: Analyze a business problem and information requirements associated with the problem.
Make a project plan to solve the problem and provide the required information service.
Use various information gathering techniques to elicit user information requirements and system expectations.
Construct and interpret a variety of system description documents using the SSAD approach.
Design the graphical user interface (GUI) forms and reports within a structured programming environment.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: The Systems Analyst and Information Systems Development
Hafta 9: Merging Process and Data Models
Hafta 10: Merging Process and Data Models
resources: Cashman, T. J., Rosenblatt, H. J., and Shelly, G. B. (2009). Systems analysis and design. 7th edition. Boston, MA: Course Technology.
Hoffer, J. A., George, J. F., and Valacich, J. S. (2011). Modern systems analysis and design. 6th edition. Upper Saddle River, NJ: Prentice Hall.
Kendall, K. E., and J. E. Kendall. (2011). Systems analysis and design. 8th edition. Upper Saddle River, NJ: Prentice Hall.
Whitten, J. L., and Bentley, L. D. (2007). Systems analysis and design methods. 7th edition. Boston, MA: McGraw-Hill.
prerequisites: CMPE 100 / CMPE 101 / CMPE 102 / CMPE 130 / COMP 112 / COMP 150 / COMP 152 / COMP 180 / MIS 102

courses.name: Business Data Communications
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a solid understanding of how data communications and networking technologies work in business environments. It also introduces emerging technologies and shows how they can be applied to create business solutions and support business objectives.
courses.description: This course presents the fundamental concepts of data communications, networking, distributed applications, and network management and security. It covers networking hardware and software, network protocols and topologies, network design techniques, local area networks (LANs) and wide area networks (WANs), the Internet, intranets and extranets, wireless networking, firewalls, cryptography, and data compression.
learning_outcomes: Communicate and discuss business related technical aspects of computer networks and telecommunications professionally.
Identify network equipment and network service requirements.
Identify the appropriate network architectures available to integrate intra- and inter-organizational business processes.
Design, implement, and upgrade computer networks for various business needs.
Evaluate, select, and develop network equipment and network service providers.
Monitor, analyze, and improve network performance.
Design, implement, enforce and update security policies and procedures for computer networks.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Computer Networks and Data Communications
Hafta 2: Fundamentals of Data and Signals
Hafta 5: Making Connections Efficient: Multiplexing and Compression
Hafta 6: Errors, Error Detection, and Error Control
Hafta 8: Local Area Networks: The Basics
Hafta 9: Local Area Networks: Software and Support Systems
Hafta 10: Introduction to Metropolitan Area Networks and Wide Area Networks
Hafta 12: Voice and Data Delivery Networks
resources: William Stallings, Business Data Communications: Infrastructure, Networking and Security, 7th Edition, Pearson, ISBN: 0-273-76916-2
Beasley, J. S. (2009): Networking. 2nd Edition. Prentice Hall.
McCabe, J. D. (2007). Network Analysis, Architecture, and Design. 3rd Edition. Morgan Kaufmann.
Dean, T. (2013). Network + Guide to Networks. 6th Edition. Course Technology.
Fitzgerald, J., A. Dennis and A. Durcikova. (2012). Business Data Communications and Networking. 11th Edition. Wiley.
Forouzan, B. A. (2013). Data Communications and Networking. 5th Edition. McGraw-Hill.
Kurose, J. F. and K. W. Ross. (2013): Computer Networking: A Top-Down Approach. 6th Edition. Prentice Hall.
Peterson, L. D. and B. S. Davie. (2012). Computer Networks: A Systems Approach. 5th Edition. Morgan Kaufmann.
Tanenbaum, A. S. and D. J. Wetherall. (2011). Computer Networks. 5th Edition. Prentice Hall.

courses.name: System Analysis and Design II
courses.purpose: This course is the second of the two system analysis and design courses offered by the Business Informatics Program. It aims to develop the analytical, technical, managerial, and social skills needed to bridge users of information systems and information technology specialists, with a strong emphasis on the role and mindset of the system analyst.
courses.description: System analysis and design covers the concepts, skills, methodologies, techniques, tools and perspectives essential for systems analysts to successfully develop business information systems. The course approaches system development from a business perspective and includes an overview of system development methodologies, followed by Object-Oriented Analysis and Design (OOAD). Students also gain first-hand experience with computer-aided software engineering (CASE) tools through running cases.
learning_outcomes: Analyze a business problem and information requirements associated with the problem.
Make a project plan to solve the problem and provide the required information service.
Use various information gathering techniques to elicit user information requirements and system expectations.
Construct and interpret a variety of system description documents using the OOAD approach.
Design the graphical user interface (GUI) forms and reports within a object-oriented programming environment.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Systems Analysis and Design
resources: Cockburn, A. (2000). Writing Effective Use Cases. Addison Wesley.
Dathan, B. and S. Ramanth. (2010). Object-Oriented Analysis and Design. Springer.
Johnson, J. (2010). Designing with the Mind in Mind: Simple Guide to Understanding User Interface Design Rules. Morgan Kaufmann.
Miles, R. and K. Hamilton. (2006). Learning UML 2.0. O'Reilly.
Satzinger, J. W., R. B. Jackson, and S. D. Burd. (2009). Systems Analysis and Design in a Changing World. 5th Edition. Course Technology. (SJB5E).

courses.name: Management Science
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to improve the knowledge and computer application skills of students in order to develop more complex ideas for management decision making.
courses.description: The course emphasizes management science tools such as linear programming, integer programming, and the transportation problem. It covers the formulation of actual problems as linear programming models, graphical solution and simplex method for solving linear programming problems, sensitivity analysis, computer solution, assignment and transportation problems, and integer and mixed-integer programming problems.
learning_outcomes: Identify business problems that could be modeled mathematically.
Build mathematical models for complex situations faced in service and production sectors using a variety of sophisticated modeling techniques such as linear programming and integer programming.
Solve these LP problems graphically and using the Simplex Method and translate the solution into courses of action for the user.
Apply sensitivity analysis to the solution and interpret the results.
Formulate the transportation problem and apply Transportation Simplex to find its optimal solution.
Identify uses and limitations of the various techniques currently available.
Develop mathematical modeling skills.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Linear Programming - Introduction and Modeling
Hafta 2: Linear Programming - Graphical Solution Procedure and Complex Problem Formulations
Hafta 3: Linear Programming - Complex Problem Formulation
Hafta 4: Linear Algebra - Systems of Linear Equations Linear Programming - Simplex Method
Hafta 10: Transportation Problem and Transportation Simplex
Hafta 11: Transportation Problem and Transportation Simplex
Hafta 13: Integer Programming - Branch and Bound Algorithm
resources: Introduction to Operations Research, F.S. Hillier and G.J. Lieberman, McGraw-Hill Higher Education, 2001.
Optimization in Operations Research, R.L. Rardin, Prentice Hall, 2000.
Operations Research: Applications and Algorithms, W. L. Winston, Duxbury Press, 1994.
An Introduction to Management Science, D.R. Anderson, D.J. Sweeney, T.A. Williams. West Publishing, 1997.

courses.name: User Interface Design
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a general appreciation of the art of user interface design, particularly graphical user interfaces in computer and software systems with a target of Full Stack Development. It also emphasizes the importance of better user interfaces for improved productivity in computer literacy.
courses.description: The course covers user interface design concepts and applications, including simple interface components, form controls, GUI and visual design, prototyping with Figma, design systems, dynamic UI, SPA basics, asynchronous UI, multi-page navigation, animations, microinteractions, accessibility, universal design, and API/data interactions.
learning_outcomes: Able to design and implement basic friendly interfaces with simple UI Components, using a modern programming language.
Able to design and implement advanced user interfaces with components such as Tree and Table.
Able to use IDE tools and manage components effectively.
Able to design and implement multi-from user interfaces.
Understand general concepts of User Interface Designing
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the concept of User Interface and its applications (HTML)
Hafta 2: Simple interface components (UI Components & Layout Foundations)
Hafta 3: Form controls, JavaScript Functions, Events, CSS Basics & Visual Hierarchy
Hafta 4: GUI and Visual Design with Cascading Style Sheets
Hafta 5: Prototyping I: Figma basics / Introduction to Databases & Data Modeling
Hafta 6: Prototyping I: Figma basics / Introduction to Databases & Data Modeling (devam)
Hafta 7: Prototyping II & Design Systems / Relational Database Design
Hafta 9: Prototyping II & Design Systems / Relational Database Design
Hafta 10: Dynamic UI & SPA (Single Page Applications) Basics SQL & CRUD Logic SQL + Full-Stack Intro
Hafta 11: Asynchronous UI & Multi-Page & SPA Navigation
Hafta 12: Animations & Microinteractions & Accessibility & Universal Design / API Mapping & Data Interactions & & Accessibility & Universal Design
Hafta 13: Final Project Presentations and reflection
Hafta 14: Final Project Presentations and reflection
Hafta 15: Final Project Presentations and reflection

courses.name: Year III Summer Practicum
courses.purpose: This practicum is designed to provide students with supervised practical experience in the field of Management Information Systems. It helps students apply what they have learned in a real organizational setting and complete the required summer internship component of the program.
courses.description: This is the second of the two summer practicums of the Management Information Systems Program. The practicum is an obligation completed during the summer following the third year of the curriculum. Students are expected to spend a minimum of three weeks at an organization in the Information and Communication Technologies (ITC) sector or at the Information Technology (IT) department of an organization and gain additional practical experience in the field of Management Information Systems. At the end of the summer practice, students should prepare and submit a report about their experiences to the Program Coordinator.
learning_outcomes: Describe an organization by the sector, industry, legal status, ownership structure and size.
Define the mission of the firm.
Analyze the task environment and primary stakeholders of the firm.
Analyze the organizational structure of the firm.
Analyze the organizational structure and business processes of the information technology (IT) department.
Design, implement, or use partial or complete system solutions.
Design, implement, optimize, or administer partial or complete databases.
resources: Coronel, C., S. Morris, and P. Rob. (2011). Database systems: Design, implementation, and management (9th Ed.). Course Technology.
Haag, S., and M. Cummings. (2013). Management Information Systems for the Information Age. 9th Edition. McGraw-Hill.
Laudon, K., and J. Laudon. (2013). Essentials of Management information systems. 10th Ed. Prentice Hall.
Dennis, A., Wixom, B. H., and Roth, R. M. (2012). Systems analysis and design, 5th edition. Wiley.
Tegarden, D. P., A. Dennis, and B. H. Wixom. (2012). Systems Analysis and Design with UML. 4th Edition. Wiley.

courses.name: Enterprise Systems
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with an understanding of enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems and their role in organizations. It also aims to develop the ability to evaluate, select, implement, and use ERP and enterprise application integration solutions effectively.
courses.description: This course covers the concepts, business processes, functions, data requirements, implementation strategies, and integration issues of ERP systems. It also examines how ERP systems support business operations and their integration with web-based technologies and enterprise application integration (EAI).
learning_outcomes: Conduct feasibility analysis to develop, buy or lease an enterprise resource planning (ERP) module or solution.
Identify the stakeholders of the ERP project, and their interests; and make necessary trade-offs among these interests.
Select an ERP or enterprise application integration (EAI) solution, or vendor.
Prepare a project plan to implement the ERP solution.
Manage ERP projects, and, organizational and technical risks of ERP implementations.
Develop high-level architectural blueprints of an ERP module or a complete ERP solution.
Identify software and process customization requirements for an ERP module or a complete ERP solution based on the needs of a particular user, department, or organization.
Use an ERP solution efficiently and effectively.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Enterprise Systems for Management
Hafta 11: Organizational Change and Business Process Re-Engineering
Hafta 12: Global, Ethics and Security Management
Hafta 13: ERP Applications: Supply Chain Management
Hafta 14: ERP Applications: Customer Relationship Management
resources: Luvai Motiwalla, Enterprise Systems for Management, 2nd ed., Pearson, ISBN: 0132145766
Ruh, W. A., F. X. Maginnis, and W. J. Brown. (2001). Enterprise application integration: A Wiley tech brief. Wiley.
Silverston, L. (2001). The data model resource book. Vol. 1: A library of universal data models for all enterprises. Wiley.
Silverston, L. (2001). The data model resource book. Vol. 2: A library of data models for specific industries. Wiley.
Silverston, L. (2009). The data model resource book. Vol. 3: Universal patterns for data modeling. Wiley.

courses.name: Management of Information Technology
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students’ understanding of how information technology can be managed strategically to support business operations, managerial decision-making, and competitive advantage. It also introduces the managerial implications of current hardware, software, and communication technologies.
courses.description: This course covers the role of information systems and information technology in organizations, including emerging technologies, social contexts, strategy, business process redesign, organizational culture and politics, implementation, and the costs and benefits of information systems. It also examines project presentations and managerial perspectives on technology adoption and information systems management.
learning_outcomes: Identify the specific business strategy and underlying system and technology strategies.
Recognize the economic, cultural, ethical and legal issues about information systems and technology.
Analyze business processes and identify the opportunities provided by the information systems and technology to redesign these processes more effectively and efficiently.
Make a detailed analysis of the costs and benefits of a specific information system, and construct multi-criterion decision making models to justify technology investments.
Monitor current trends in information technology and make informed decisions about sourcing information systems.
Identify the organizational risks and potential issues that may result in resistance-to-change when a new system or technology will be introduced to the firm.
Organize the information technology department and manage information processes and resources of a company.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Emerging Technologies for Information Systems
Hafta 3: Social Contexts of Information Systems
Hafta 5: Using Information Systems to Reinvent Strategy
Hafta 6: Using Information Systems to Rethink Business Processes
Hafta 10: Organizing and Positioning Information Systems Activities
Hafta 14: The Costs and Benefits of Information Systems
resources: Laudon, Kenneth C., Laudon, Jane P. Management Information Systems: Managing the Digital Firm, 16th Edition, Pearson, 2019, ISBN: 9780135191798
Boddy, D., Boonstra, A., and Kennedy G. Managing information systems: Strategy and organization. 3rd Edition. Harlow, UK: Prentice Hall-Financial Times Press
Brown, C. V., DeHayes, D. W., Hoffer, J. A., Martin, W. E., and Perkins, W. C. Managing information technology. 7th Edition. Prentice Hall.
Applegate, L. M., R. D. Austin, and D. L. Soule. Corporate information strategy and management: Text and cases. 8th Edition. Boston, MA: McGrawHill.
Clarke, S. Information systems strategic management. 2nd Edition. London, UK: Routledge.
Frenzell, C. W., and Frenzell, J. Management of information technology. 4th Edition. Boston, MA: Course Technology.
Galliers, R. D., and Leidner, D. E. (eds.). Strategic information management: Challenges and strategies in managing information systems. 2nd Edition. Oxford, UK. Butterworth-Heinemann.
Lucas, Jr., H. C. Information technology: Strategic decision-making for managers. Hoboken, NJ: Wiley.
McNurlin, B., Sprague, R., and Bui, T. Information systems management in practice. 8th Edition. Upper Saddle River, NJ: Prentice Hall.
Pearlson, K. E., and Saunders, C. S. Strategic management of information systems. 4th Edition. Hoboken, NJ: Wiley.
Schiesser, R. IT systems management. 2nd Edition. Upper Saddle River, NJ: Prentice Hall.
Ward, J., and Peppard, J. Strategic planning for information systems. 3rd Edition. Hoboken, NJ: Wiley.

courses.name: Information Systems Project I
courses.purpose: This course is the first of the two information systems project courses. The aim is to expose the students to the system development life cycle and information technology (IT) project management with the help of a live and working project.
courses.description: This course introduces students to the system development life cycle and information technology project management through a live and working project.
learning_outcomes: Describe the basic project management concepts.
Have knowledge about Project Management Institute's project management methodology.
Plan a project taking the related knowledge areas into account (scope, time, cost, risk, communication, quality, human resources and procurement).
Become an intermediate level user project management software.
Present the project effectively to its primary stakeholders.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Project Management and Information Technology Context
Hafta 4: Project Management Process Groups: A Case Study
resources: A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge, 4th ed, ISBN-13: 978-1933890517
prerequisites: CMPE 100 / CMPE 101 / CMPE 102 / COMP 112 / COMP 150 / MIS 102

courses.name: Management Support Systems and Business Intelligence
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the principles and use of management support systems and business intelligence tools for improving decision-making in organizations. It also aims to help students understand how different decision-making needs can be supported by appropriate systems and AI-based techniques.
courses.description: This course examines the principles, categories, development and use of information systems that support management decisions at all levels in an organization. It covers decision making styles and managerial needs, and relates them to management support systems such as data-, model-, knowledge- and document-driven DSS, executive support systems, group decision support systems, and Web-based DSS. The course also includes artificial intelligence applications such as expert systems, fuzzy logic, pattern recognition, genetic algorithms, artificial neural networks, data-, text- and Web-mining, and intelligent software agents. It concludes with the justification and development of MSS and future business intelligence trends.
learning_outcomes: Identify the decision making needs of individuals or groups at an organization.
Use various management support systems (MSS) and business intelligence (BI) software packages.
Propose the appropriate decision-making aids or artificial intelligence (AI) tools to solve a specific organizational or managerial problem.
Develop a BI architecture.
Analyze, design, and implement MSS and BI applications.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Decision Support Systems (DSS) and Business Intelligence (BI)
Hafta 2: Decision Making, Systems, Modeling, and Support
Hafta 3: Decision Support System (DSS) Concepts, Methodologies, and Technologies
Hafta 5: Data Preparation and Data Exploration
Hafta 6: Data Warehouses and Data Marts
Hafta 12: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Expert Systems (ES)
Hafta 14: Collaborative Computer-Supported Technologies and Group Support Systems (GSS)
resources: Goodwin, P. and G. Wright. (2004). Decision Analysis for Management Judgment. 3rd Edition. Wiley.
Groebner, D. F., P. W. Shannon, P. C. Fry, and K. D. Smith. (2011). Business Statistics: A Decision-Making Approach. 8th Edition. Prentice Hall.
Russell, S. J., and P. Norvig. (2010). Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach. 3rd Edition. Upper Saddle River, NJ: Prentice Hall.
Sauter, V. L. (2010). Decision Support Systems for Business Intelligence. 2nd Edition. Wiley.
Vercellis, C. (2009). Business Intelligence: Data Mining and Optimization for Decision Making. West Sussex, UK: Wiley.
Winston, W. L. (2004). Operations Research: Applications and Algorithms. 4th Edition. Cengage.
prerequisites: BUS 274 / EC 272 / FM 212 / FM 301 / HEC 214 / IE 232 / MATH 240 / MATH 301 / PSY 214 / SOC 214

courses.name: Information Systems Project II
courses.purpose: This course is the continuation of MIS 497 Information System Project I. It aims to expose students to the system development life cycle and information technology (IT) project management through a live and working project.
courses.description: Continuation of the previous project course focused on completing a live group project. The course covers project development activities, progress reporting, project management practices, formal documentation, and post-completion maintenance, update, and upgrade planning.
learning_outcomes: Practice PMI methodology of project management.
Practice stakeholder management.
Complete the group project started at the previous course (MIS 497).
Practice formal document creation for project management.
Plan for maintenance, update and upgrade activities after project completion.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Finalization of project groups and subjects.
Hafta 3: Groups work on their projects and report their progress.
Hafta 4: Groups work on their projects and report their progress.
Hafta 5: Groups work on their projects and report their progress.
Hafta 6: Groups work on their projects and report their progress.
Hafta 8: Groups work on their projects and report their progress.
Hafta 9: Groups work on their projects and report their progress.
Hafta 10: Groups work on their projects and report their progress.
Hafta 11: Groups work on their projects and report their progress.
Hafta 12: Groups work on their projects and report their progress.
Hafta 13: Groups work on their projects and report their progress.
Hafta 14: Groups work on their projects and report their progress.
resources: UML for the IT Business Analyst: A Practical Guide to Object-Oriented Requirements Gathering, Howard Podeswa
The Business Analyst's Handbook, Howard Podeswa
Use Case Driven Object Modeling with UMLTheory and Practice, Doug Rosenberg, ISBN-13: 978-1590597743
Success in Your Project: A Guide to Student System Development Projects, Philip Weaver, ISBN-13: 978-0273678090

courses.name: e-Commerce
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide an understanding of issues involving the Internet and electronic commerce, including technological directions, the nature of Internet marketing, and the strategies, structures, and processes used in this new business environment. It also examines the emergence of the digital economy and explores the economic and social impacts of electronic commerce.
courses.description: The course covers the Internet and electronic commerce, e-business and e-commerce concepts, the digital economy, e-business models, e-marketplaces, electronic service quality, website features, digital marketing, customer relationship management, web analytics, online payment, online security systems, and logistics in e-commerce.
learning_outcomes: Define most important e-commerce business and revenue models.
Summarize the impact of Internet and e-commerce on various industries.
Discuss consumer behavior in the online medium.
Explain advances in mobile commerce.
Develop an e-commerce business plan.
Defend his/her e-business ideas and projections.
Recognize business opportunities in the online medium.
Improve their decision making process by following the innovations and developments in digital world.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: The New Economy, E-business, E-commerce, Internet and globalization. First-Mover Advantages. E-Business Hype: Myths and Realities
Hafta 2: New Business Rules in e-Commerce Redefining the Market Redefining the Competitors Redefining the Products Redefining the Customers: Attitudes and behaviors of digital customers

Hafta 3: Managerial and Technical Aspects of Initiating E-commerce. Establishment of e-commerce site site, domain name selection, website hosting services. Selection criteria in determination of utilization of in house programming or utilization of e-commerce solution packages.
Hafta 4: Web born companies vs. traditional companies which start to use web as an alternative sales channel. Main differences between their strategies.

Hafta 5: E-Business Models: Evaluation of e-business models according to their main theme, role of the participants, their revenue model and the value added to the user Analysing the e-business models like B2C, B2B, C2C etc.
Hafta 6: E-marketplaces E-tailer Virtual merchant Portal Content provider Transaction broker Market creater Service provider Community provider
Hafta 8: Electronic Service Quality (E-SQ) E-Recovery Quality (E-RecS-QUAL) What needs to be done to increase the quality of e-service.
Hafta 9: Web site features. Links, text, banners and the content of the site Product categorization, links, ad slots
Hafta 10: Dijital Marketing 1 SEO, SEM, Organic and paid traffic,
Hafta 11: Digital Marketing 2: Affiliate Marketing, Content marketing. Word of mouth marketing (WOMM), advergaming, gamification
Hafta 12: Customer Relationship Management,E-CRM, Social CRM
Hafta 13: Web Analytics, Concepts of Data, Information and Knowledge. Data warehousing and Data Mining
Hafta 14: Online Payment Online Security Systems
resources: Efraim Turban; (2010) Electronic Commerce, 6th Edition; Prentice Hall
E-Commerce by Kenneth C. Laudon and Carol Guercio Traver; 2013; 9th Edition; Prentice Hall
